observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended Management to direct the following:
1.22.1 	a. Internal Audit Division to:
Develop and enforce internal controls to monitor inventory access, prevent theft or misuse, and ensure the accuracy of data.
Establish clear, standardized procedures for inventory handling, including receiving, storing, issuing, and updating inventory records.
b. General Services Office
Use reliable software or systems to track inventory in real time, allowing for accurate, up-to-date records of all stock movements.
Consider the use of barcode scanners or RFID tags to automate inventory tracking, reducing human error and improving accuracy.
Provide training for staff on proper inventory management practices, including accurate record-keeping, stock handling, and adherence to procedures.
To account the missing inventories as stated above.
c. Inventory Committee
Conduct periodic physical counts (i.e beyond the semestral count) of inventory and reconcile them with recorded amounts to identify and address discrepancies.
d. City Accounting Office
Improve Reporting and Documentation. Ensure that inventory reports, such as stock cards, ledgers, and inventory counts, are consistently updated and properly documented for accurate financial reporting.
In the interim, while awaiting the full implementation of the RAMP System, assign a dedicated staff member to manage the task of updating the supply ledger cards in accordance with the approved internal inventory management policy.

Recommendations:

a. The CHLMO to prepare the required documents and regularly furnish the same to CACCO for recording and updating purposes;
b. The CGSO to furnish the CACCO the copies of the document for any special patent of the parcels of land for the City Government of Tagum to facilitate the immediate recording in the books; and
c. The City Accountant to regularly update the records to reflect the accurate balance of the Land accounts in the financial statements, as well as disclose the actual status of land properties in the notes to financial statements.

Recommendations:

3.23.1 a. The LRAM Committee consider utilizing the City Engineering Office’s Quantum Geographic Information System as a starting point for classifying the recorded Local Road Network.

c. The City Accounting Office fully disclose the pertinent information outlined in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2018 in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the City Treasurer to:
Prepare a confirmation from the depository banks on the details of Debit Memo and Credit Memo. In case there are items that could not be identified, require the bank to make a certification that these were correctly posted in the account of the city government. Subsequently, these documents will serve as bases of CACCO in recording the appropriate journal entries.
Furnish CACCO with the original Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents on stale checks necessary for the preparation of the Journal Entry Vouchers following the procedures to ensure recording of such cancellation.
Design a system to ensure that checks prepared are timely delivered to respective creditors.
Make use of Transmittal Letters on release of checks to Liason Officers. Also, only a copy of Disbursement Voucher should be attached for the signature of Payee in order to avoid lost.
Direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments immediately on the identified book errors.  Also, facilitate the preparation of monthly reconciliation of the Cash-in-Bank accounts with the records of CTO.

Recommendations:
Direct the Local Finance Committee to trace the responsibilities and obligation of City Government to the Source Agency in order to warrant the reversion to General Fund.
Also, direct the City Accountant to trace the corresponding Trust Liability on these accounts to fully support the reversion to General Fund proper and subsequently be used to fund priority projects.

Recommendations:
Direct the City Treasurer to prioritize and expedite the process of securing the necessary documentary requirements for the closure of account and subsequent transfer to its existing AGDBs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to evaluate the submitted request for write-off and determine the extent of possible credit loss of the city government and recommend the amount for impairment loss allowance to the Local Chief Executive.
Direct the City Accountant to immediately record in the book of accounts the duly approved LFC recommendation.
Likewise, direct the Program Administrator to expedite the submission of required documents to facilitate the request for write-off of dormant accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2019-002 dated March 20, 2019.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of actions:
Direct the Inventory Committee to formulate strategies or techniques in the conduct of physical count during a pandemic or similar situation in order to render timely reports that will serve as basis in the reconciliation of accounts.
Direct the CGSO and the City Accountant to devise a system to reconcile their inventory records periodically to determine the causes of the deficiencies and disparity in the account balances for proper disposition within reasonable time.
Direct the Internal Audit Division to recommend policy that will ensure that all items transferred to the City Government are fully accounted and reported.
Direct the CGSO to be the central unit or the sole authorized department to receive all items procured and transferred to the city government.

Recommendations:
We recommended to:
Direct the City Engineer to submit reports with supporting documents on the demolished properties (including appraisal report from COA) to CACCO in order to derecognized the appropriate property account
Also, on properties transferred without cost, direct the CGSO to submit the Sanggunian Panlungsod Resolution authorizing the transfer, acceptance report of the end-user, and Project Cost Sheet to CACCO for the derecognition of accounts.
Fast track the implementation of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The City General Services Office and City Engineering Office to submit the Utilization Report and the Certificate of Completion & Acceptance and all other necessary supporting documents, respectively, of the above-mentioned completed projects to the city accounting office;
The City Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account and compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended to direct the Local Finance Committee to use the appropriate budgetary accounts for expenditures on meals and snacks and the City Accountant to appropriately use the prescribed account title for the expenditure.

We recommended that the CGSO and CEO submit the Utilization Report and the CCA, respectively, to the CACCO of all completed projects to cause the transfer of or reclassify said projects from CIP accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the CACCO require the implementing departments to submit a report on the status of the 20% DF Projects labeled as for verification and take appropriate action and/or require necessary documents if found completed.

We further recommend that based on the adjusted data, the CACCO compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Office, City Accounting Office, and the City Engineering Office be required to conduct a reconciliation of the documents maintained by their respective offices to determine the correct classification of each PPE account and thereafter, effect reclassification and adjustments, where necessary, to ensure the validity and accuracy of the records.

Likewise, we recommended that the offices concerned strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008. 

We also recommended that the City form a team to address and comply with the requirements set under COA Circular No. 2015-008. If necessary, require said team to attend seminar/training on Local Road Asset Management.





Finally, we recommended that the City Accounting Office properly disclosed the relevant information for the PPE such as: (a) PPEs that were impaired or lost, (b) carrying amount of temporarily idle PPEs, (c) fully depreciated PPEs that are still in use, (d) the carrying amount of unserviceable PPEs and held for disposal, and (e) the total local road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
The City GSO and City Accounting have already conducted reconciliation of the said PPE Accounts. 

However, there are still three (3) remaining PPE accounts which are still to be reconciled.

Initially reviewed PPE items subject for reclassification.

As to the findings on LRAM, the City is currently formulating the team to address concerns and requirements for local road network management. An Executive Order has already been drafted for the formulation of said team. (Please see attached) Said draft EO is currently at the City Planning and Development Office for reconciliation with other existing EOs concerning same matter. 

As to the recommendation for attendance to LRAM trainings and seminars, the recommendation is duly noted and the City is very much willing to send participants. However, due to the crisis brought by COVID-19, we cannot assure your end that the team can participate to such trainings this year.





Initially identified the PPEs to be disclosed in the year-end Financial Reports.
observations.reason:
Implementation was disrupted during the 2nd quarter due to the Covid-19 pandemic.















PPEs are still being identified to be disclosed in the year-end Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the CGSO and CEO submit the Utilization Report and the
CCA, respectively, to the CACCO of all completed projects to cause the transfer of or
reclassify said projects from CIP accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the CACCO require the implementing departments to
submit a report on the status of the 20% DF Projects labeled as for verification and take
appropriate action and/or require necessary documents if found completed.

We further recommended that based on the adjusted data, the CACCO compute
for the required depreciation on the completed projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management require the:
1) CSWDO to:
 submit copy of the uploaded SAC data and the SAC forms of the 1,848
beneficiaries; otherwise, the total amount of P11,088,000.00 shall be
suspended in audit; and
 submit justification on the 24,668 mis-targeted beneficiaries specifically
the 20,692 with wrong supporting documents; 2,910 with lacking data
presented in their SAC forms; and the 1,066 unqualified beneficiaries;
2) Three (3) LGU Tagum workers to refund the P18,000.00 cash subsidy
received; otherwise, the same would be charged to those responsible for their
inclusion;
3) CSWDO/Punong Barangays concerned to submit their respective certification
for the 127 recipients beneficiaries that: they belong to a low-income family;
do not belong to a 4Ps household; none of the member(s) of his/her family
falls within the exclusion; and how much monthly amount of honorarium they
receive from their respective barangays;
4) cash subsidy of 934 recipients who are private employees to submit
certification from their respective employers/DOLE that: they are “no work no
pay” during the ECQ periods; and that they did not receive any cash subsidy
under the DOLE amelioration program;
5) Recipient two (2) pensioners to submit SSS/CSWDO certification that they
did not receive any pension during the ECQ periods; and
We also recommended that management maintain an updated database of Tagum
City residents through better coordination with Puroks and Barangays to properly identify
possible beneficiaries of social amelioration programs of the government and accurately
expedite the delivery of the same.

We recommended that the LCE remind its BAC to strictly comply with the
requirements on the use of NPEC, specifically on the required submission of copy of
NOA and proofs of posting said NOA and Contract/PO.
We also recommended that the LCE remind its CACCO to include on its checklist
of basic documentary requirements for NPEC the copy of Mayor’s or Business Permit,
OSS, Income/ Business Tax Return, as the case may be, to be submitted by the selected
suppliers; and the NOA and its proof of posting.
We further recommended that the LCE remind its BAC to submit to the Audit
Team the required copy of Mayor’s or Business Permit, OSS, and Income/Business Tax
Return, as the case may be, of the respective suppliers.

We recommended that the LCE remind its BAC to strictly comply with the
requirement that the PO should duly be received first by the concerned supplier
concerned prior to any delivery for both parties to have a perfected contract incorporating
the terms and conditions governing the procurement and corresponding delivery.
We also recommended that the CGSO require the supplier concerned to sign the
PO as to its acceptance of the terms and conditions prior to the receipt of any delivery.

We recommended that the BAC at all times see to it that the NOA and PO,
including the NTP, if necessary, be posted in: (i) the PhilGEPS website; (ii) the website
of the City and (iii) any conspicuous place in its premises within ten (10) days from the
issuance of said documents so as to facilitate full transparency and timeliness.
We also recommended that the BAC require its personnel responsible for posting
the NOA and PO to check first the completeness of said documents as to supplier’s
“conforme” and the necessary dates before proceeding with the required posting.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC restrict the TACEMPCO from participating in
the City’s procurement activities unless the HOPE, BAC members, BAC-TWG, the BAC
Secretariat, the Head of the Project Management Office, or the end-user unit, and the
project consultants, if any, are not members of said cooperative.

We recommended that the City Mayor approve the DVs by himself whenever
local funds are disbursed and delegate only those for regular recurring administrative
expenses such as payrolls for regular or permanent employees, expenses for light, water
telephone and telegraph services, remittances to government creditor agencies such as
GSIS, SSS, LBP, DBP, National Printing Office, Procurement Service of the DBM and
others where the authority to approve may be delegated.
We also recommended that the personnel delegated with the authority to approve
DVs and related documents strictly observe the limits provided for and refrain from
signing checks which duty is vested with the City Administrator.
We further recommended that the City Administrator perform his function as
counter-signatory to the disbursement checks of the City but at the same time refrain
from signing as approving officer in the disbursement vouchers unless he is no longer the
BAC Chairman.

We recommended that the City formulate written guidelines concerning the
receipt, storage, repacking, releasing, and distribution of its relief goods detailing therein
the persons and/or offices responsible and the policies and procedures to be observed and
followed.
We also recommended that the City DRRM Council incorporate the written
guidelines concerning the receipt, storage, repacking, releasing, and distribution of relief
goods in its CDRRMP.
We further recommended that the CGSO initiate the request for budget allocation
concerning the construction of additional warehouses sufficient and suitable enough to
accommodate all the City’s inventories including relief goods of great quantities.
Finally, we recommended that the CGSO, CDRRMO, and CSWDO submit the
distribution lists for the unsubstantiated distribution of 4,331 sacks of rice, 63,033 cans of
sardines/canned goods, and 2,965 packs of biscuits, milk, etc. as detailed in the AOM.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Secure from the Sangguniang Panlungsod a resolution granting him
authority to accept the P68,423,800.86 donations from various sources for
Covid-19-related purposes, to ratify the contracts of donations entered into
by him in behalf of CGT;
b) Instruct the City’s responsible officials that all payments, including
donations, in favor of CGT shall be receipted immediately by the CTO;
c) Instruct the personnel authorized by him or the CDRRMO and CBO to
facilitate the use of the Cash Donation Utilization Request (CDUR) before
any cash donations are utilized as required under Annex A of COA Circular
No. 2014-002;
d) Instruct the City’s responsible officials that payments out of the cash
donations shall be made through the issuance of check based on duly
approved disbursement vouchers pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002;
e) Instruct the CBO to prepare and maintain the Registry of Cash Donations
and Utilization (RCDU) to record the donations per donor per purpose as
required under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
f) Instruct the CGSO and CSWDO to submit the lacking duly acknowledged
distribution lists for the P1,123,980 welfare goods, P1,696,798.00 food
180
supplies, and P3,458,551.90 office, medical and other supplies distributed as
detailed in Annex A of this AOM.
We also recommended that the CACCO:
a) Demand from the Donations and Sponsorship Team/Admin and Support
Group of the CCTF the liquidation of the P786,557.00 cash donations
claimed to have been already utilized;
b) Prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for "Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF" (Annex C of COA Circular No. 2014-002) for cash donations
received amounting to P100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while
those below P100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as
"Various Donors" per purpose;
c) Recognize in the books the P295,000.00 donated PPE as detailed in Annex A
of this AOM and accordingly, prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for
all donated PPE;
d) Disclose in the Notes to FS a) the P812,000.00 total cash donations received;
b) the correct amount of P68,272,420.86 in-kind donations received; c) the
total inventories procured, issued, and balances sourced out from the BGCM
funds and cash donations; and d) the PPE procured out of the Bayanihan
Grant and cash donations as well as the donated PPE and the disposal or
transfer, if any;
e) Refrain from recognizing in the books in-kind donations but disclosed only
the same in the Notes to FS; and
f) Demand from the CLYDO the accounting of cash donations it collected, if
any, which was coursed through the private accounts of one its job order
personnel.
We further recommended that the CGSO:
a) Prepare the AR for In-Kind Donations for DRRM (Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2014-002) and have the same signed by the City Mayor or his
authorized representative and the donor for every receipt of in-kind
donations;
b) Prepare and issue PARs for the P295,000.00 donated PPE as well as the ICS
for those P114,697.00 small items with useful life of more than one year,
copy furnish the Audit Team; and
c) Submit to the NDRRMC, through the Office of the Civil Defense, a one-time
report showing separately the cash donations and in-kind donations received
by the City for the Covid-19.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the LCE direct its offices:
 CACCO – to record all supplies and materials intended for stocking under the
appropriate Inventory accounts upon delivery and expense the same only after
the presentation of RIS, SSMI, ICS and Distribution List, whenever
applicable.
 CGSO – to submit the RIS as well as the SSMI, and ICS, if applicable, of
those supplies and materials already issued.
 CGSO and CDRRMO - to submit the:
o Distribution List of those supplies and materials already issued; and
o Inventory report for those supplies and materials not yet utilized, if any.

We recommended that the LFC observe the standard template for the RFUSI for
PPAs under BGCM as required by DBM LBC No. 125 and revise and/or incorporate the
necessary information for a more transparent reporting.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CEO, particularly the project
engineers, and CARCHO to religiously observe the posting requirements on project
billboards specifically as to billboard specifications, billboard posting at the project site,
and the updating of billboard information during the construction period as stated under
COA Circular No. 2013-004. Otherwise; the cost of billboards not within the meaning of
said Circular shall be disallowed in audit as the case may be.
We also recommended that the City Mayor remind its City Information
Technology Unit to strictly observe the required quarterly posting in the City’s official
website of the updated status of implementation of the projects funded under the 20%
DF.

We recommended that the LCE remind its offices concerned, particularly the
CEO, CHLMO, and CENRO, to fast track the implementation of the infrastructure
projects under the 20% DF.
We also recommended that the CEO give priority on the road concreting and
asphalting projects by sourcing out sufficient allocations for such projects and regularly
monitor the condition of the City roads and provide immediate maintenance works, if
warranted, using the standards of DPWH.
We further recommended that the City Internal Audit Services monitor and
evaluate the implementation of the City’s infrastructure projects and accordingly,
produce a report pertaining thereto as part of its performance audit.
Finally, we recommended that the City consider implementing some projects by
contract, if necessary, to fast track the completion and delivery of services to the intended
beneficiaries.

 expedite the completion of the PAMANA projects particularly on the
reconstruction of the damaged portion of the perimeter fence in view of the

We further recommended that the CGT implement its projects by contract, as
necessary, to fast track the completion and delivery of services to the intended
beneficiaries.

Recommendations:

the projects “Construction of TCCHC” and the “Construction of TOTIT Module 3 with

We also recommended that the CGT management consider implementation of
some of infrastructure projects by contract, as may be necessary, to fast track the

We further recommended that the City Mayor require its CBO explain the P6.88
Million disbursements in excess of appropriations for the projects Constructions of both

Finally, we recommended that the CACCO adjust the P60,375.00 office supplies,

Recommendations:

We also recommended that the City’s responsible officials post the updated CYs

We recommended that the City Accounting Office strictly screen the granting of cash advances by requiring the accountable officers to liquidate their previous cash advance, if any, before granting additional cash advance and thereafter, issue the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” which is to be made an integral part of the supporting documents.


We also recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before releasing the check to the accountable officer concerned.


We likewise recommended that the City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s Office see to it that only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers.


It is also recommended that the City Accounting Office issue demand letters to those accountable officers with long-overdue cash advances and subsequently furnish the Audit Team of copies of the same.

Finally, we recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office require those offices with cash advance requirements to have their own disbursing officers, duly bonded and properly aware of the accountability that comes along with the cash advances entrusted to them.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the General Services Office, City Accounting Office, and the City Engineering Office be required to conduct a reconciliation of the documents maintained by their respective offices to determine the correct classification of each PPE account and thereafter, effect reclassification and adjustments, where necessary, to ensure the validity and accuracy of the records.
Likewise, we recommended that the offices concerned strictly observe the accounting and reporting requirements for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the City form a team to address and comply with the requirements set under COA Circular No. 2015-008. If necessary, require said team to attend seminar/training on Local Road Asset Management.
Finally, we recommended that the City Accounting Office properly disclosed the relevant information for the PPE such as: (a) PPEs that were impaired or lost, (b) carrying amount of temporarily idle PPEs, (c) fully depreciated PPEs that are still in use, (d) the carrying amount of unserviceable PPEs and held for disposal, and (e) the total local road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office strictly screen the granting of cash advances by requiring the accountable officers to liquidate their previous cash advance, if any, before granting additional cash advance and thereafter, issue the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” which is to be made an integral part of the supporting documents.
We also recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before releasing the check to the accountable officer concerned.
We likewise recommended that the City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s Office see to it that only permanently appointed officials and employees are designated as disbursing officers.
It is also recommended that the City Accounting Office issue demand letters to those accountable officers with long-overdue cash advances and subsequently furnish the Audit Team of copies of the same.
Finally, we recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office require those offices with cash advance requirements to have their own disbursing officers, duly bonded and properly aware of the accountability that comes along with the cash advances entrusted to them.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City require the recipient NGOs/POs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them. If necessary, impose sanctions to the delinquent NGOs/POs, as provided in Section 9.0 of COA Circular 2007-001.
Also, we recommended that the City ensure that all provisions in COA Circular 2007-001 are complied with especially in the preparation of the Memorandum of Agreement between the NGOs/POs and LGU-Tagum City which shall contain all the required provisions, particularly in the specific period of liquidation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Engineering Office replace the posted billboard and comply with the sample template presented in COA Circular 2013-004 and install the same to a strategic place duly visible to the public to afford the latter of the essential features/elements of the project.
We also recommended that the City comply appropriately with the posting of reports on fund utilization and status of project implementation in three (3) conspicuous public places and in the official website of the City
Finally, we recommended that the LCE send a written notice to the Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriation and Senate Committee on Finance upon posting of the reports on the City’s website.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Engineering Office update the information provided in the billboard and transfer/install the same to a place where it is visible to properly inform the public about the project.
Also, we recommended that the City Social Welfare and Development Office nominate a third party CSO/NGO/PO to monitor the implementation of the project as required in the PAMANA Manual of Operation 2017 under the Transparency and Accountability Mechanism (TAM).
Finally, we recommended that Management instruct the City Information System Development Unit to post the related PAMANA project on the official website of the City to comply with the TAM.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Engineering Office expedite the implementation and completion of the project and justify the incurrence of additional labor cost totaling P783,549.25 which resulted from the delayed completion of the same.
We also recommended that the City Government of Tagum consider implementing some projects by contract, if necessary, to fast track the completion and delivery of services to the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City General Services Office and the City Engineering Office submit the Utilization Report and the Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, respectively, to the City Accounting Office (CACCO) of all completed projects to cause the transfer of said projects from CIP accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts.
Moreover, we recommended that the CACCO require the implementing departments to submit a report on the status of the 20% Development Projects labeled as for verification and take appropriate action and/or require necessary documents if found completed.
Finally, we recommended that based on the adjusted data, the City Accounting Office computes for the required depreciation on the completed projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and stop the charging of expenditures not allowed under the 20% Development Fund. Otherwise, the recurrence of similar charges against the 20% DF shall be disallowed in the audit.
We also recommended that the City Development Council identify and prioritize projects chargeable against the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City of Tagum.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (CDRRMO), refrain from procuring goods charged against the 30% Quick Response Fund prior to the declaration of the City under the State of Calamity.
We also recommended that the CDRRM Council, through the CDRRMO, immediately accomplish the requisites for the issuance of the SP Resolution for the declaration of the State of Calamity in times of disasters, when warranted.
We also recommended that the BAC and the City Accounting Office strictly ensure that complete and appropriate basic documentary requirements are complied with in all transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the officials concerned, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee, observe the consolidation of purchase requests for similar items, for similar purposes, and which are to be used on or about the same time so as to comply with the requirements of the procurement law.
We also recommended that the officials concerned to submit a written justification why the purchase requests were not consolidated and subjected to small value procurement instead of public bidding.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Health Office (CHO) and the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office submit the duly received/signed distribution lists of the drugs and medicines amounting to P5,564,529.75.
We also recommended that the General Services Office submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued together with the Requisition and Issue Slips covering the drugs and medicines issued to the CHO.
Relevantly, we recommended that the City Accounting Office henceforth record the drugs and medicines procured for distribution under the account “Drugs and Medicines for Distribution” and see to it that expensing of the same in the books is duly supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office record all supplies and materials intended for stocking under the appropriate Inventory accounts upon delivery and expense the same only after the presentation of the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), and Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS), whenever applicable.
We also recommended that the General Services Office submit the RIS as well as the ICS, as applicable, of those supplies and materials already issued.
We likewise recommended that the City Accounting Office record the supplies and materials costing P1,770,462.50 not yet distributed to end-users as at year-end to the appropriate Inventory for Distribution accounts, otherwise submit the corresponding RIS and the ICS when already distributed.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office record all construction materials intended for infrastructure projects under the Construction Materials Inventory upon delivery and recognize the same into the appropriate Construction in Progress account upon issuance to end-users after the presentation of the corresponding Requisition and Issue Slips and the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
We also recommended that the General Services Office submit the Requisition and Issue Slips pertaining to the P2,948,363.07 construction materials directly recorded in the Construction in Progress account.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office immediately submit the “Duly Received Distribution List” supporting the distribution of the P1,508,000.00 construction materials.
We also recommended that the City Accounting Office verify the existence of all inventories presented in the year-end financial statements by requiring the submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories from the City General Services Office.
We likewise recommended that the City General Services Office include in its physical count all outstanding inventories presented in the year-end financial statements and present the same in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the responsible officials of the City give full attention in the eventual construction of a Sanitary Landfill the soonest time possible to comply with the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 in order to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City, through the City Environment and Natural Resources Office ensure the establishment, operation, and monitoring of a functional Materials Recovery Facility in all its twenty-three (23) Barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accounting Office to properly deduct withholding tax on professional fees, talent fees and similar payments to professionals and other recipients not otherwise subject to withholding tax on compensation as required under subsection (A) of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the officials responsible for the processing and eventual disbursement of the City Government’s funds stop the appropriation and application of the same for religious and/or private purposes; otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit.
We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee sees to it that the “No Contact Rule” as provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 is strictly followed.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management ensure that all the data needed to be completely indicated in the submitted Quarterly Report on Publicized Government Projects, Program and Activities.
We also recommended that Management conduct regular meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess and/or monitor whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Avoid lump-sum appropriation and identify specific PPAs in the AIP and allocation of budget to be funded under explicit areas of development/office indicating the corresponding project cost, necessary fund flows to approximate the reasonable timing in the release of funds and period of implementation; Direct the offices concerned to prepare the necessary POWs for implementation of the P26.11M appropriated projects which remained unimplemented; Require the barangays to strictly comply with the submission of the BDPs to serve as a tool in project identification of the city; Require the CPDO, CARCHO and CEO and other offices concerned in the implementation to improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate timely completion of the projects. observations.reason: Thirty-four (34) development projects totaling P17.3 Million under 20% Development Fund (DF) remained unimplemented while fifty-three (53) development projects totaling P34.3 Million were not fully implemented as of year-end. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should: Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance to enable the City to implement projects related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the scheduled timeframe; Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of livelihood; Avoid “un-programmed” appropriations and identify specific PPAs in the LDRRMFIP to approximate the reasonable timing in the release of funds and period of implementation.
observations.reason: LDRRM Fund for CY 2018 amounting to P58,202,226.91 was not utilized. Likewise, several projects remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Strengthen the SWM IEC through implementation of the specific programs/ projects enumerated under the SWMP of the City; Revisit those programs and projects which are not viable and find alternative means to achieve the identified targets; Enjoin all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of the SWMP and have functional MRFs and emphasize the prompt submission of required reports. As a consequence, non-complying barangays may not be given their financial aid allocation; Consider addressing all the recommendations noted by the survey respondents on the garbage collection procedures of the CENRO; and Enforce strictly the segregation at source to ensure compliance with the SWMP.
observations.reason: Observation on collection and disposal of waste revealed that garbage collectors were not equipped personal protective equipment. Likewise, some barangays do not have fully operational MRF. Also, the same did not submit a waste diversion report.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the LCE require the Inventory Committee to conduct
proper planning in undertaking the physical count of PPE and inventories
recorded in the books of the City, so as not to compromise the submission of the
RPCPPE to be submitted before January 31 of the succeeding year;
2. Also, we recommended that Management submit the RPCPPE for CY 2018 to the
Office of City Auditor;
3. Moreover, we recommended that department heads assigned to monitor the status
of implementation of the trust fund projects shall prepare a status report on Trust
Fund projects and submit to the Local Chief Executive as basis for the latter to
issue a Certificate of Completion and Acceptance for completed Trust Fund
projects; and
4. Finally, we recommended that the City Accountant consider the transfer of PPE
accounts from the trust fund to the general fund and recognize depreciation
expenses in each PPE account.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that Management require the recipients-NGOs/POs to
immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them. If necessary, impose
sanctions to the delinquent NGOs/POs, as provided in Section 9.0 of COA
Circular 2007-001;
2. Also, we recommended that Management require the accreditation of the
NGO/PO and submission of documentary requirements to Sangguniang
Panlungsod to be eligible for the fund transfer; and
3. Finally, we recommended that Management communicate with the Local
Chief Executives of the Provinces of Bohol and Leyte as well as the Medical
Center Chief of Southern Philippines Medical Center, and request for the
liquidation of the funds transferred to them.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the department head assigned to monitor the status of
implementation of the projects shall prepare a status report on BUB projects
and submit to the Local Chief Executive as basis for the latter to issue a
certificate of completion and acceptance for completed BUB projects.
2. We also recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the balances of
Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities accounts with the reports prepared by the
department head who monitors the status of BuB projects, and return the
unexpended balances to the respective source agencies and/or declare the
unexpended LGU counterpart balances as savings and revert to
unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management shall:
1. Submit written justification for undertaking the projects by administration
knowing that the prerequisite conditions/cases provided under Section 3.3 of
Appendix 1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 were not present when the
City implemented the projects;
2. Submit the relevant documents to support that the City is capable of
implementing the projects by administration such as experience or track
record relevant to the projects, list of professional, technical & experienced
personnel to undertake, supervise and manage the projects, list of equipment
owned by the City to be used in the projects;
3. Submit a copy of Approved Annual Procurement Plan showing the inclusion
of the projects to be undertaken by administration; and
4. Submit written authority from the President for the project undertaken by
administration costing P190.7 Million and written authority from the
Secretary of DPWH for the projects costing P20 Million to P50 Million,
otherwise the disbursements/expenditures shall be suspended or disallowed in
audit as the case maybe.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the City appropriate expenditure items that are allowed
to be charged to the 20% DF and that management adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, otherwise, recurrence of charges
of such expenditures against the 20% DF shall be disallowed in audit.
2. We also recommended that the City Development Council shall select and
prioritize projects chargeable against the 20% Development Fund in
accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, to attain the
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the
City of Tagum.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall instruct the CEO
particularly the project engineers to religiously install the billboards and the
City Architect’s Office (CARCHO) to follow the required specifications of
tarpaulins/billboards for infrastructure projects as stated under COA Circular
No. 2013-004; and
2. Also, we recommended that Management shall post in their official website
the development projects under the 20% DF to give the public access to
information and promote full transparency of PPAs undertaken by the City.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
1. Refrain from charging against the LDRRMF projects and activities that are
not included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under item 3.0 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, be guided with the provisions of
DILG JMC No, 2013-1 as to the appropriate utilization of the LDRRMF,
specifically with regard to the projects and activities for each thematic areas of
disaster management enumerated therein; and
2. Refund from the General Fund to the Trust Liability – DRRM account the
amount of P1,139,265.46 representing the utilized amount of the LDRRMF
for PPAs that do not fall within the disaster related activities under the law
and statutes.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
1. Instruct the City General Services Officer to conduct an ocular inspection of all
the vehicles owned by the City and implement the required markings of “For
Official Use Only” in prescribed size of three (3) inches with the name of the
City under it.
2. Submit a listing all motor vehicles of the City and indicating therein whether
or not each vehicle has been marked with the “For Official Use”, otherwise
fuel consumption of unmarked vehicles shall be disallowed in audit.
3. Ensure that the 11 unmarked vehicles and the two (2) vehicles with tiny
markings cited in this AOM shall be prioritized for markings in the prescribed
size.
4. Require motor vehicles to be kept in the garage of the City when not in use and
after office hours except when being used for strictly official business outside
office hours.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by
filling the mandatory positions in keeping with item 5.0 of JMC 2014-1 to assure
implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and
management.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office
(CDRRMO) furnish the said reports to the offices concerned upon receipt from the City
Accounting Office (CACCO), pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure compliance with Republic Act 2003,
particularly in the collection and handling of solid wastes.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management:
1. Strictly ensure that all job order employees reporting at the new city hall to
register their daily attendance on the biometric machine;
2. Maintain an updated logbook for every department/office that will be regularly
checked/inspected by the HRMO;
3. Require the employees assigned on the field to use the bundy clock for the
establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the
computation of their wages; and
4. Ensure that employees who leave the office, either for personal or official
reasons secure a duly signed pass slips from their supervisor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management strictly observe the timely submission of
report at the time set by COA Circular No. 2014-01 for the timely evaluation of GAD-
related accomplishments.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the management of the City Economic Enterprises
(CEE) look into the apparent subleasing of stall and acquiring or renting more
than one (1) stall in the market and come up with specific measures to solve
the problem;
2. We likewise recommended that the management of CEE endeavour to
improve the cleanliness and sanitation of the public market by imposing
sanctions, if necessary; and
3. Also, we recommended that provisions in the Code shall be implemented by
the CEE management such as requiring stallholders and helpers/laborers to
pin their identification cards, prescribe the size and design of stall signboards
and require the stallholders to provide fire extinguishers, among others.

We recommended that the Management:
1. Develop a structured Monitoring and Evaluation system with appropriate tools,
and a reporting system on the progress/status of PPAs using performance
indicators, and targets and baselines, where applicable, with an analysis of
expected outputs, objectives and goals; and
2. Conduct regular meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess
whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned to ensure that
implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that CARCHO expedite the preparation of POW for 20%
Development Fund infrastructure projects in the succeeding year;
2. We also recommended that Management should avoid unprogrammed
appropriations or lump-sum appropriations and identify specific PPAs in the
AIP, as well as allocate the budget to be funded under explicit areas of
development/office by indicating the corresponding project cost, necessary
fund flows to approximate the reasonable timing in the release of funds and
period of implementation;
3. Moreover, we recommended that Management should require the CPDO,
CARCHO and CEO and other offices concerned in the implementation to
improve the planning, scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to
facilitate the timely completion of the projects. Submit a concrete time-plan to
serve as a basis in gauging actual accomplishments while planning of the
projects to be implemented should be completed before the start of the year;
and
4. Finally, consider implementing some projects by contract, if necessary, to fast
track the completion and delivery of services to the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the LCE form a fact-finding committee to conduct
investigation on the destruction/damages made on Crisis Center Building
at Barangay Magdum and determine the persons responsible and
accountable, subject to administrative and criminal liability and instruct
the committee to furnish COA a copy of their final report;
2. Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the SMO to provide a
security personnel to safeguard the Crisis Center Building and all
identified infrastructure projects not yet occupied and turned-over to end-
user/department;
3. Moreover, we recommended that the CEO prioritize and expedite the
completion of the Crisis Center Building at Barangay Magdum and for
offices concerned, GAD and CEO, to continue to monitor the repairs on
damaged parts and its completion;
4. Finally, we recommended that the LCE consider implementing some
projects by contract, if necessary, to fast track the completion and delivery
of services to the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the implementing department concerned make a
thorough study and identify projects that are feasible and not include projects
which had history of failure to avoid cancellation/dropping of projects;
2. We recommended that Management amend its purchase request and
coordinate with the supplier to prioritize the delivery of the specific kind of
Bamboo (Kawayang Tinik/Tongcan), to CSO beneficiaries in order that the
BUB project may be implemented;
3. We also recommended that the offices concerned handling the BUB project to
coordinate with CSO beneficiaries and procure the exact specifications of
requested materials so as not to delay the implementation of the BUB project;
and
5. Finally, we recommended that the unexpended balance of dropped/cancelled
BuB projects be returned to the source agency as soon as possible.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) of the City conduct a
meeting with the CVO, as the implementing department and the MNLF
organization as beneficiary of the PAMANA program, and require from the
organization a strategic/contingency plan to sustain the program;
2. We also recommended that the LCE remind the MNLF members/beneficiaries
to religiously abide by the provision stated in the MOA and project proposal
especially on the matter of re-dispersing the original stocks if an offspring had
been produced and require them to submit financial reports and status of
dispersed animals; and
3. Also, we recommended that the CVO as implementing department, to
continue to assess, monitor and make preventive and corrective actions to
sustain the life of the remaining dispersed animals with the MNLF
beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
We reiterate the prior year’s recommendations that the management should:
1. Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation
and prior years’ unexpended balance to enable the City to implement projects
related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the
scheduled timeframe;
2. Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible
loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of
livelihood;
3. Submit a detailed Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team for
proper evaluation of the activities conducted and projects implemented by the
CDRRMO.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Management:
1. Utilize available workforce to assist in the repacking of goods to expedite the
distribution of relief goods; and
2. Ensure that designated evacuation centers comply with the minimum
requirements.

Recommendations:
We reiterate the CY 2015 recommendations to be undertaken by the City:
1. We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a local tax ordinance
which includes specific guidelines and policies in determining contract price,
cost of the project and classification of services offered by the Asphalt and
Concrete Batching Plant;
2. We also recommended that the Asphalt Marketing and Advisory Committee,
when dictated by specific circumstances propose to the Sangguniang
Panlungsod any revision on matters relating to the costing and selling prices
of the goods offered by the Asphalt and Concrete Batching Plant;
3. We further recommended that the ACBOS furnish the COA Office and CACCO
with the list of unpaid balance of accounts of customers from Private, NGOs,
NGAs and LGUs on completed projects with its corresponding certificates of
completion and the actual cost incurred classified per HMA products for CY
2017 and 2018, to facilitate recording thereof to appropriate accounts;
4. We likewise recommended that the CACCO shall record the revenue and
income from the asphalt operations in conformance with PPSAS No. 9; and
5. Lastly, we recommended that the City Treasurer require each payee to present
an order of payment/billing statement from the CEO as basis to collect the
specific amount due to the City and open a separate bank account for the
Asphalt and Concrete Batching Plant as provided in Section 9.1 of City
Ordinance No. 13.s-2001.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the City Economic Enterprises (CEE) Manager shall
initiate a re-measurement of all stalls in the public market and use the actual
measurement of the area in the computation of the rental rate per sq.m./day, to
be fair to all stallholders concerned;
2. We also enjoined the CEE Manager to look into the current stall rental
rate/sq.m./day used for each section of the market and make the necessary
adjustments, as applicable;
3. Finally, we recommended that the CEE shall endeavor to install a computer
system that could simplify the processes involved in maintaining a data base
for all the relevant information about the 1,080 stallholders such as name of
lessees and other personal information, date of award of stall, history of
transfers from former to current lessees, stall number, market section, actual
area in sq.m., rental rate/sq.m./day, rental payments ledger and other pertinent
information, including a link to the location map where a particular stall is
located.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the City Economic Enterprises (CEE) Manager shall
set a target to reduce the stall rental receivables and strategize on how to
improve its collection efficiency;
2. We also recommended that the CEE Manager shall endeavor to promptly
issue statements of accounts or demand letters as soon as accounts become
due and demandable;
3. We likewise recommend that the CEE Manager shall look into the
circumstances where stallholders whose contracts of lease were not renewed
still continue to operate their businesses at the public market.

Recommendations:
1. We recommended that the Economic Enterprise Manager shall recommend to
the City Mayor and the Board for the approval of the Sangguniang Panlunsod
to modify and include in the Contract of Lease the prescribed amount of rental
rates per square meter/day and the total area leased by stallholders/lessees in
the different sections of the public market for clarity and transparency as well
as to avoid misunderstanding and ambiguities; and
2. We also recommended that the Economic Enterprise Manager ensure to fill in
the date and place the raffle of the stall was conducted, as applicable, in the
space provided in the contract, to complete the information required.

We recommended that the GSO prepares and submits the SSMI in accordance with Section 121 of NGAS and conducts periodic physical count thereof; We also recommended that the CACCO and CGSO make the reconciliation of records to appropriately adjust/take-up issuances of affected inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee conducted physical count and reconcile the records with GSO and CACCO on June and October 2018.
observations.reason: The GSO is currently on the process of installing its new electronic inventory system that started on October 2018, as of year-end December 31, 2018, the linking of the inventory system from warehouse to records division of GSO is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all cash advances granted to Special Disbursing Officers for purposes other than their personal and official travelling expenses and payment of payrolls shall be recorded under the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in order to establish the complete accountability of the said SDO as of a specific period.
Stop granting cash advances to officials and employees thru an authorized personnel or thru SDO.
Travelling expenses of employees should be granted thru individual cash advances to establish the accountability of each travelling officer/ employee.
observations.managementAction: An office order was issued to alternate signatories of disbursement vouchers by the City Treasurer to ensure that cash advance for travel shall be granted thru individual officers and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all individuals performing collecting functions for and in behalf of the City Treasurer shall be duly authorized in writing being his deputies as mandated under RA 7160.
observations.reason: As of year-end December 31, 2018, out of 101 collecting personnel in the CEE0 only three (3) were deputized and have authorization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management facilitate the submission of the list of beneficiaries with proof of acceptance or receipt of goods/services for the BuB projects/programs amounting to P2,064,896.74 and P1,818,956.80 for CYs 2017 and 2016, respectively, otherwise Notices of Suspension shall be issued for said transactions.
We also recommended that Management require the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) to prepare the Status and Monitoring Reports on the physical implementation of all programs/projects funded by DSWD and furnish copies to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The department assigned to monitor and evaluate BUB programs had complied and submitted the complete list of beneficiaries with proof of acceptance or receipt of goods/services for the BuB projects/program to COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management facilitate the submission of the list of beneficiaries with signatures signifying proof of acceptance or receipt of goods/services for the Skill Training projects of MCCT-IPs totaling P1,463,647.00, otherwise Notices of Suspension shall be issued for said transactions; and We also recommended that Management require the City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) to prepare the Status and Monitoring Reports on the physical implementation of all programs/projects funded by DSWD and furnish copies to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management, as implementing agency of PAMANA Program, should install project and community billboards in all five (5) barangays where the program are implemented as required in the implementing guidelines and MOA of PAMANA Program for CY 2016; and Also, we recommended that Management should instruct the CISDU to post the related programs/projects/activities of the City in the official website of the City, so as to fulfill the transparency requirements in all government transactions.
observations.managementAction: Tarpaulins and Billboards of PAMANA program were already installed in all five (5) project site.
observations.reason: The development projects for 20% Development Fund were not posted on Official Website of the City. Thus, audit recommendation No. 2 is reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management explain and justify why the MNLF person/representative was the one who identified the beneficiaries of the PAMANA Program instead of the LGU representative; and We also recommended that Management shall abide not only with the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between DA and LGU but also with the Guidelines in the Implementation of the PAMANA Program for FY 2016.

We recommended that Management require the supplier to submit the lacking compliance certifications and other requirements related to the delivery of cattle and goats. Otherwise, the disbursement shall be suspended in audit; We recommended that Management submit a written justification relative to the loss of the goats stating therein the preventive measures undertaken, post-action on the implementation the program and action taken against the supplier; and We recommended that Management revisit the MOA on the PAMANA Program to properly implement the program to the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the Audit Team to the PAMANA program revealed that, mortality on the dispersed animals is still existing in CY 2018. Thus, audit recommendation No. 2 is reiterated under Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management that all procurement shall pass the usual mode of procurement under RA No. 9184 and all payments must be made by check and paid directly to the suppliers; We also recommended that payments made to the supplier shall be deducted/withheld with the mandatory taxes as prescribed under BIR rules and regulations; We likewise recommended that reimbursement of expenses shall only be made in small amounts and paid out of the Petty Cash Fund in keeping with the Imprest Fund System.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund monthly in order for the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the offices concerned on the required dates, pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management direct the Head of LDRRMO to prepare and submit an Annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan in accordance with Republic Act 10121.
We also recommended that Management submit a comprehensive Implementing Plan containing the courses of actions before, during and after the calamities/disasters.
We also recommended that Management submit a detailed Accomplishment Report for CY 2017 to the Audit Team for proper evaluation of the activities conducted and projects implemented by the CDRRMO.
observations.reason: Annual City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan was submitted however, mechanism for the timely delivery of relief goods was not provided therein. Moreover, report on the status of implementation/completion of some projects funded under the 5% Calamity Fund was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all the procurements shall pass the usual mode of procurement under R.A. No. 9184 and all payments must be made by check and paid directly to the suppliers; We also recommended that payments made to the supplier shall be deducted/withheld with the mandatory taxes as prescribed under BIR rules and regulations; We likewise recommended that reimbursement of expenses shall only be made in small amounts and paid out of the Petty Cash Fund in keeping with the Imprest Fund System.

We recommended that the City Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to record the fund transfer as Due from NGAs (1-03-02-021) and ensure that all subsequent transfers should be recorded as receivable from the IA, subject to liquidation thereof.
We also recommended that the City Mayor communicate with the Dep-Ed – Region XI and require them to submit the Report of Disbursements certified correct by their Regional Accountant, approved by the Head and duly audited by the Auditor of the IA.

We recommended that Management prioritize and make necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the projects/programs so that the CSO beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits, avoid the incurrence of penalty deductions and project termination; We also recommended that the offices concerned CAGRO and CVO together with the CSO beneficiaries make necessary actions to comply with the documentary requirements needed for the implementation of the BuB projects/programs; and Finally, we recommended that the unexpended balance of dropped/cancelled BuB program/projects shall be returned back to the source agency as early as possible.
observations.reason: BUB-Project funded by BFAR amounting P3.0 Million were not fully implemented at year-end CY 2018. Thus, audit recommendation is reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that claims for RATA of the city officials and employees conform to the above-mentioned rules and regulations. We recommended that the Office of the SP Secretariat submit a list of regular sessions conducted during the month indicating the actual attendance and absences incurred by the SP Members to the Human Resource Management Office to serve as their basis in payroll preparation. We recommended that the officials concerned refund the overpaid RA.
observations.reason: As of year-end December 31, 2018, neither official receipt nor any documents attesting the payment of P50,625.00 representing the payment by City Officials on the overpayment of RATA made on CY 2017, was received by Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that Management make necessary actions to facilitate the implementation of the projects/programs so that the CSO beneficiaries may enjoy the benefits, avoid the incurrence of penalty deductions and project termination; 2. We also recommended that the offices concerned, PESO and CAGRO together with the CSO beneficiaries to make necessary actions to comply with the documentary requirements needed for the implementation of the BuB projects/programs; and 3. Otherwise, the disbursements shall be suspended in audit; 2. Also, we recommended that the unexpended balance of those completed BuB programs/projects, shown in the table on Status of National Fund Transfer from National Government Agencies for Bottom-Up-Budgeting CY 2015 (Annex G) shall be returned to the source agency; and 3. We recommended that the members of the Local School Board, in the preparation of the School Board budget, give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Section 272 of the RA 7160. Refrain from using the SEF for expenses not prescribed under the law.
We reiterated the prior year recommendation that the City establish the CDRRMO and hire qualified personnel as required under RA 10121 and JMC Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.reason: The LGU already passed City Ordinance No. 802, S-2017 "An ordinance approving the 2017 reorganizational structure, staffing pattern and qualification standards of the City Government of Tagum" which included the creation of CDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: The CACCO and the CEO shall reconcile their records to determine the status and correct balances of the affected CIP accounts (Refer to Annex R for the details of completed project lodged in CIP accounts); The CGSO shall submit Utilization reports and CEO shall provide the CACCO with Certificate of Completion and Acceptance of all completed projects to cause the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; Based on the adjusted data, the City Accounting Office compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
observations.reason: P99.6 Million completed Development project under 20% DF remained in CIP as of year-end December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and continuously send demand letters to all concerned to enforce collection of the dormant accounts; 2. We also recommended that the Accountant take appropriate action for accounts which balances remained inactive or non-moving in the books of accounts for 10 years or more and where settlement or collectability is remote by requesting for write-off of these accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; 3. We likewise recommended that the Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the impairment loss on receivables in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.reason: A task force has been created (E.O. 2015-045) which resulted to settlement of some accounts. Request for write-off was sent to City Auditor’s Office subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the concerned personnel submit the DRLQR to the CACCO together with its supporting documents for appropriate recording in the City’s books of accounts. 2. Designate the CSWDO as the lead office in extending assistance based on the guidelines under functions and duties of the CSWDO, and as provided under Section 483 of R.A. No.7160. 3. Conduct a thorough evaluation as to the eligibility of recipients and make appropriate validation of documents submitted. 4. Limit the grant of assistance as dole outs and prioritize development programs and projects to livelihood promotions and job opportunities with the marginalized sector as beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Of the P5.06M distributed materials, the amount of P1.01M remained in the inventory account as of 12.31.2018 despite issuance thereof to beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the CVO trace all mortalities of dispersed animals and comply with the documentary requirements for the request of relief from accountability thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to be submitted to the Commission on Audit. Locate the beneficiaries of the dead animals and create a working group together with other offices such as the General Services Office, Legal and CACCO who will facilitate preparation of documents supporting the request for relief. 2. We likewise recommended that strict monitoring and complete documentation of all dispersals shall be made to avert any similar occurrence in the future. 3. We also recommended that the CACCO reverse the entry made to write-down the inventory and instead provide for disclosures in the financial statements as to actual condition of the agricultural produce under the said account. 4. Finally, we recommended that the CACCO and CVO reconcile their records, investigate the causes of difference and submit a report on the reconciliation made.
observations.reason: Ongoing reconciliation of CVO and CACCO records. The complete documents supporting the request for relief were not yet forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that transactions must be recorded in accordance with the assigned codes and account titles as indicated in the Revised Chart of Accounts and prescribed for use by government agencies of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS), for uniformity and facility in consolidation and easy monitoring of budgetary allocations per object of expenditure. 2. We also recommended that the City Accounting Office and the City Budget Office determine and identify the specific nature of accounts in order to reconcile the balances of accounting and budget records and resolve differences between both records. 3. We likewise recommended that the Budget Office specifically indicate the expense account titles in coordination with the Accounting Office for activity-related budget and not lump expenses under MOOE account so as both offices shall use uniform expense accounts for the same transactions.

We recommended that the CGSO request authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to the respective beneficiaries and prepare the Invoice Receipts (IR) - Gen. Form No. 30-A to support the Certificate of Turnover and Acceptance of Completed Projects to be received by the beneficial owners. We also recommended that the City Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher/s (JEV/s) for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books with the following supporting documents: (1) Authority from the SP; (2) JEV of the beneficiaries taking up the PPEs received by them; (3) Invoice Receipt (IR) and (4) Certificate of Turnover and Acceptance of Completed Projects duly received by the recipients. Based on the adjusted data, the City Accounting Office compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
observations.reason: The LGU already passed SP Resolution No. to facilitate the transfer without cost of PPEs to identified NGAs/LGUs. The City Mayor stated that they will first repair/ renovate other identified PPEs before transferring. For school buildings, the LGU will accordingly retain it in their books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a local tax ordinance which includes specific guidelines and policies in determining contract price, cost of the project and classification of services offered by the Asphalt and Concrete Batching Plant. We also recommended that the AMAC, when dictated by specific circumstances, propose to the Sangguniang Panlungsod any revision on matters relating to the costing and selling prices of the goods offered by the Asphalt and Concrete Batching Plant. We likewise recommended that the Asphalt Concrete and Batching Plant Section furnish the CACCO with a list of unpaid balance of customers whether private or government on completed projects with its corresponding certificate of completion to facilitate recording thereof to appropriate accounts. We further recommended that the CACCO record the receivable and income accounts on the asphalt operations in conformance with PPSAS No. 17. Lastly, we recommended that the City Treasurer require each payee to present an order of payment from the CEO as a basis for them to collect the specific amount due the City.
observations.reason: CACCO already recorded the receivable & the CTO required presentation of an Order of Payment before issuance of OR. Formulation of guidelines & procedures for incorporation in the City Ordinance is on-going. Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the LCE to require the City Architect to explain why he failed to undertake/prepare the detailed engineering activities and documents required under Annex “A” of the Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184. We also recommended that the City adhere to proper engineering procedures and construction methods in the implementation of projects either by contract or by administration/ force account.

We recommended limiting the granting of honoraria only to members of committees/councils who are not paid salaries or per diems and those authorized specifically by law, rules and regulations. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE consider appointing other equally competent official/employee to be a member of the committee/council to avoid an official/employee being overworked and instead increase his/her productivity. Furthermore, we recommended that the concerned officials/employees settle the disallowances that will be imposed therefrom.
observations.reason: The concerned officials and employees identified as persons liable already submitted their appeal to COA – Regional Office No. XI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate the immediate approval by the Sangguniang Panlungsod on the proposal of availing services for payroll payment through ATM provided by its government depository bank for both its casual and job order personnel.
observations.reason: The payroll payment through ATM for job-order individuals program base were not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to follow-up the issuance of Credit Notice/s at COA Central Office. Also, for cash advances of more than 30 days compel the accountable officers to submit the liquidation report or enforce refund thereof.
observations.managementAction: The persons responsible made a follow-up to the COA Central Office in order to hasten the issuance of the Credit Notices.
observations.reason: Management had made constant follow-up at COA Central Office for the release of the Credit Notice.

      taking; and

      inventory accounts.

expenses and payment  of  payrolls  shall  be  recorded  under  the  account

thru individual cash  advances  to  establish  the  accountability  of  each

in behalf of the City Treasurer shall be duly authorized  in  writing  being
his deputies as mandated under RA 7160.
