We recommended that the management  refrain  from  using  the  SEF  for  the
implementation of the different ALS program/activities.

We reiterate the recommendations stated in the CY 2016 AAR:

   1. We recommended that Management prioritize and make  necessary  actions
      to facilitate the implementation of the projects/programs so that  the
      CSO beneficiaries may enjoy the  benefits,  avoid  the  incurrence  of
      penalty deductions and project termination;


   2. We also recommended that the offices concerned CAGRO and CVO  together
      with the CSO beneficiaries make necessary actions to comply  with  the
      documentary requirements needed for  the  implementation  of  the  BuB
      projects/programs; and


   3.   Finally,   we   recommended   that   the   unexpended   balance   of
      dropped/cancelled BuB programs/projects shall be returned back to  the
      source agency as early as possible

We recommended that Management:

   a. Avoid lump-sum appropriation and identify specific PPAs in the AIP and
      allocation  of  budget  to  be  funded   under   explicit   areas   of
      development/office  indicating   the   corresponding   project   cost,
      necessary fund flows to  approximate  the  reasonable  timing  in  the
      release of funds and period of implementation;


   b. Direct the  offices  concerned  to  prepare  the  necessary  POWs  for
      implementation of the P26.11M  appropriated  projects  which  remained
      unimplemented;


   c. Require the barangays to strictly comply with the  submission  of  the
      BDPs to serve as a tool in project identification of the city;


   d. Require the CPDO, CARCHO and CEO and other offices  concerned  in  the
      implementation  to  improve  the  planning  and  scheduling  of   work
      activities and manpower in order to facilitate  timely  completion  of
      the projects. Submit a concrete time-plan  to  serve  as  a  basis  in
      gauging  actual  accomplishments.  Planning  of  the  projects  to  be
      implemented should be completed before  the  start  of  the  year  for
      implementation;


   e. Consider implementing some projects by contract, if necessary, to fast
      track  the  completion  and  delivery  of  services  to  the  intended
      beneficiaries.

We recommended that management should:


1.     Maximize  the  utilization  of  the  LDRRMF   from   current   year’s
      appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance to enable  the  City
      to implement projects related to disaster prevention,  mitigation  and
      preparedness within the scheduled timeframe;


2.          Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM  to  prevent
      possible loss of life, damage to government and private property,  and
      loss of livelihood;


3.          Avoid “un-programmed” appropriations and identify specific  PPAs
      in the LDRRMFIP to approximate the reasonable timing in the release of
      funds and period of implementation.

We recommended that Management:

   a.  Strengthen  the  SWM  IEC  through  implementation  of  the  specific
      programs/ projects enumerated under the SWMP of the City;


   b. Revisit those programs and projects which  are  not  viable  and  find
      alternative means to achieve the identified targets;

   c. Enjoin all barangays to actively participate in the implementation  of
      the SWMP and have functional MRFs and emphasize the prompt  submission
      of required reports. As a consequence, non-complying barangays may not
      be given their financial aid allocation;

   d. Consider addressing  all  the  recommendations  noted  by  the  survey
      respondents on the garbage collection procedures of the CENRO; and


   e. Enforce strictly the segregation at source to ensure  compliance  with
      the SWMP.

We recommended that the local finance committee  revisit the  provision  on
the imposition of the RCEPF and SF on every  transaction  and  to  recommend
appropriate measure to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for possible  modification
of the said provision in the tax ordinance.


We recommended that the City Mayor re-issue or update the sharing  agreement
for the collections made by the Barangays inasmuch as there is no  available
document supporting such agreement.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that claims for RATA  of  the
city officials and  employees  conform  to  the  above-mentioned  rules  and
regulations.


We recommended that the Office of  the  SP  Secretariat  submit  a  list  of
regular  sessions  conducted  during  the  month   indicating   the   actual
attendance and absences incurred by the SP Members  to  the  Human  Resource
Management Office to serve as their basis in payroll preparation.


We recommended that the officials concerned refund the overpaid RA.

We  recommended  that  Management   communicate   with   the   Supplier   to
replace/install the spare parts and accessories of the Rescue Vehicle  which
did not conform with the specifications stipulated in the PO;


Also, we recommended that Management require the  Supplier  to  comply  with
the obligation for the warranty of either one percent (1%)  retention  money
or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%)  of  the
contract price or equal to P20,130.00 as  stated  in  Section  62.1  of  the
Revised IRR of RA 9184 and for further compliance  of  the  City  with  such
provision in the future procurement transactions entered into by  the  City;
and

We recommended that  the  authorized  Inspectorate  Team  shall  religiously
inspect, verify and validate the actual delivery of  goods  against  precise
and accurate specifications stipulated in the PO.

We enjoined Management to continue to remit premium contributions  to  GSIS,
Philhealth and Pag-ibig as well as the loan amortizations to GSIS  and  Pag-
ibig on the prescribed due dates; and


We requested Management to instruct the HRMO  and  the  City  Accountant  to
facilitate the remittance of the contributions and loan  repayments  of  Job
Order personnel  within  the  prescribed  due  dates  to  avoid  unnecessary
penalties for the delays incurred.

We recommended that Management instruct  the  randomly  selected  job  order
personnel to comply for head count and personal appearance at the Office  of
the Auditor anytime within the month of March 2018.


We likewise recommended  that  the  department  heads  concerned  to  submit
justification for the non-appearance of the job order personnel assigned  at
their offices.

      requirements; and

   c)  Notice to every organization which did not apply,  encouraging  them
      to apply for accreditation.

intended and expenditures which are administrative  in  nature  be  properly
charged against the regular budget or funds of the office concerned and  not
against the Local Disaster Risk Response Management Fund.

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that the City observe strictly the provisions  set  forth

   of Interior and Local Government and the Department of Public Works  and
   Highways.

2.  We also recommended that the City remit the unexpended amount after  the
   project completion to the DPWH and refrain from treating the  unexpended
   amount as savings to be used for other projects.

We  recommended  that  the  members  of  the  Local  School  Board,  in  the
preparation  of  the  School  Board  budget,  give  priority  to   programs,
activities and projects enumerated in Section 272 of the  RA  7160.  Refrain
from using the SEF for expenses not prescribed under the law.

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that the Management must direct the City  Accountant  and
   all concerned to see to  it  that  all  transactions  like  procurements
   and/or projects must  not  be  paid  by  the  local  government  if  not
   supported with complete documentations in order to avoid  suspension  of
   the  same.  Moreover,  disbursement  vouchers  and  all   its   required
   supporting document ought to be submitted  to  COA  within  reglementary
   period, otherwise, the disbursement shall be suspended in audit.


2.  The City Mayor, being the immediate  and  primary  responsible  for  all
   financial transactions and operations entered into by the City of Tagum,
   ensures that laws, rules and  regulations  shall  be  strictly  complied
   with.

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that Ms. Eva E. Estabillo ceases to act or resign as  CSO
     representative and/or officials of Rural Improvement Club,  Tagum  City
     Inc.


2.    Ensure that the Sangguniang Panlungsod shall consistently require  CSO
   beneficiaries the sworn affidavit of the Secretary of  the  NGO/PO  that
   none of its incorporators, organizers,  directors  or  officials  is  an
   agent of or related by consanguinity or affinity up to the fourth  civil
   degree to the officials of  the  GO,  otherwise  said  NGO/PO  shall  be
   disqualified from the grant of the fund.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the City  Administrator  cease  to  act  as  an  officer
approving the disbursements voucher on various procurement of the City.

Recommendations:

1.   We recommended  that  the  City  comply  with  the  provisions  of  COA
   Circular  Nos.  2013-004  and  2016-003  and  disseminate  such  to  all
   department heads or offices.

2.  We also recommended that a focal person  be  designated  to  review  and
   consolidate the reports submitted by each department  before  submission
   to COA in hard and electronic copies.

Recommendation:

We  recommended  that  the  City  Officials  and  concerned  Offices   fully
implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment  of  a
sanitary  landfill,  for  the  benefit  of  the   constituents   and   their
environment.

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that LPRAT should properly assess, evaluate and  identify
   the prospective CSO applicant to the BuB  projects/programs  and  should
   consistently require  CSO  applicant  to  comply  with  the  documentary
   requirements enumerated in the Section 4.4 of COA Circular No.  2007-001
   on requisites for entitlement to receive government funds; and


2.  We also recommended  that  Management  and  LPRAT  cancel/terminate  the
   project of those CSOs enumerated in Annex P, due  to  non-submission  of
   Financial Report to GO and COA and shall return the  unexpended  balance
   of the project to the source agency (DOLE).

Recommendation:

We reiterate the prior year  recommendation  that  the  City  establish  the
CDRRMO and hire qualified personnel as  required  under  RA  10121  and  JMC
Circular No. 2014-1.

Recommendations:

1.   We recommended that the BAC of the procuring entity should comply  with
   Section 6.8.3.3 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-50 and Section 5.12
   of JMC DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC No. 7 and invite observers in the  procurement
   process of BuB-DILG-LGSF projects/programs.


2.   We also recommended that the BAC be innovative in  sending  invitations
   to concerned and/or prospective observer by fastest means through  email
   and facsimiles.

Recommendations:

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendations that:

1. The CACCO and the CEO shall reconcile  their  records  to  determine  the
   status and correct balances of the affected CIP accounts (Refer to  Annex
   R for the details of completed project lodged in CIP accounts);


2. The CGSO shall submit Utilization  reports  and  CEO  shall  provide  the
   CACCO with Certificate of Completion  and  Acceptance  of  all  completed
   projects to cause the transfer of these projects from CIP accounts to the
   appropriate PPE accounts; and


3. Based on the adjusted data, the City Accounting Office  compute  for  the
   required depreciation on the completed projects, and  accordingly  adjust
   the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that the CTO furnish the CACCO with  the  certified  list
   of taxpayers and with the details of the annual estimated Real  Property
   and Special Education Tax Receivables to enable  them  to  validate  the
   recorded amounts of receivables.

2.  We also recommended that  the  offices  concerned  endeavor  to  verify,
   trace and establish the accurate balances of RPT and SET receivables  to
   ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the accounts.

3.   We likewise  recommended  that  the  City  provide  a  budget  for  the
   enhancements of the computer systems of offices concerned  for reliable
   and efficient services.

Recommendations:

1.   We  recommended  that  the  Accountant  conduct  regular  and  periodic
   verification,  analysis,  and  validation  of  the  existence   of   the
   receivables and continuously send demand letters  to  all  concerned  to
   enforce collection of the dormant accounts;

2.   We also recommended that the Accountant  take  appropriate  action  for
   accounts which balances remained inactive or non-moving in the books  of
   accounts for 10 years or more and where settlement or collectability  is
   remote by requesting for write-off of these  accounts  pursuant  to  COA
   Circular No. 2016-005;

3.   We likewise recommended  that the Accountant  prepare  the  necessary
   adjusting entry to recognize  the  impairment  loss  on  receivables  in
   accordance  with  the  Philippine  Public  Sector  Accounting  Standards
   (PPSAS).

Recommendations:

1.  We recommended that the concerned personnel  submit  the  DRLQR  to  the
   CACCO together with its supporting documents for  appropriate  recording
   in the City’s books of accounts.

2.  Designate the CSWDO as the lead office in extending assistance based  on
   the guidelines under functions and duties of the CSWDO, and as  provided
   under Section 483 of R.A. No.7160.

3.  Conduct a thorough evaluation as to the eligibility  of  recipients  and
   make appropriate validation of documents submitted.

4.  Limit the grant of assistance as dole outs  and  prioritize  development
   programs and projects to livelihood  promotions  and  job  opportunities
   with the marginalized sector as beneficiaries.

Recommendations:

1.   We recommended that City  General  Services  Office  (CGSO)  conduct  a
   physical count of PPE under  the  Grass  Roots  Participatory  Budgeting
   Process Fund and submit a report thereon;

2.  We recommended that the City General  Services  Office  (CGSO)  complete
   and enhance their property records and coordinate with Accounting  Office
   in eliminating the unreconciled difference between their records; and


3.  We also recommended that the focal person from  different  offices  like
   PESO and CAGRO shall coordinate with  the  CGSO  to  identify  those  PPE
   distributed  to  CSO  beneficiaries  for  proper   inventory-taking   and
   recording.

Recommendations:

   1. We recommended that CACCO shall require thorough review  and  analysis
      of all outstanding accounts payable and revert to the general fund  if
      warranted, payables which are outstanding for two (2) years  or  more,
      pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and


   2. We recommended that the  CACCO  consistently  recognize  and  book  up
      liabilities which are bonafide  obligations  of  the  agency  such  as
      services already rendered and goods delivered.

Recommendations:

1.   We recommended that the CVO trace all mortalities of dispersed  animals
   and comply with the documentary requirements for the request  of  relief
   from accountability thereof pursuant to Section 151 of COA Circular  No.
   92-386  to  be  submitted  to  the  Commission  on  Audit.  Locate   the
   beneficiaries of the dead animals and create a  working  group  together
   with other offices such as the General Services Office, Legal and  CACCO
   who will facilitate preparation of documents supporting the request  for
   relief.

2.   We  likewise  recommended   that   strict   monitoring   and   complete
   documentation of all dispersals shall  be  made  to  avert  any  similar
   occurrence in the future.

3.  We also recommended that the CACCO reverse the entry made to  write-down
   the inventory and instead  provide  for  disclosures  in  the  financial
   statements as to actual condition of the agricultural produce under  the
   said account.

4.  Finally, we recommended that the CACCO and CVO reconcile their  records,
   investigate the  causes  of  difference  and  submit  a  report  on  the
   reconciliation made.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the Accountant classify  the  receivable  accounts  aged
less than one (1) year under current assets while receivables aged over  one
(1) to more  than  10  years  under  non-current  assets,  for  purposes  of
financial statements presentation.

Recommendation:

We recommended that transactions must be recorded  in  accordance  with  the
assigned codes and account titles as indicated in the Chart of Accounts  and
prescribed for use by government agencies through  COA  Circular  No.  2015-
009, for uniformity and facility in consolidation  and  easy  monitoring  of
budgetary allocations per object of expenditure.

Recommendations:

1.   We recommended that transactions must be recorded  in  accordance  with
   the assigned codes and account titles as indicated in the Revised  Chart
   of Accounts and prescribed for use by government  agencies  through  the
   Manual for the Philippine Public Sector  Accounting  Standards  (PPSAS),
   for uniformity and facility in  consolidation  and  easy  monitoring  of
   budgetary allocations per object of expenditure.

2.  We also recommended that the City Accounting Office and the City  Budget
   Office determine and identify the specific nature of accounts  in  order
   to reconcile the balances of accounting and budget records  and  resolve
   differences between both records.

3.  We likewise recommended that the  Budget  Office  specifically  indicate
   the expense account titles in coordination with  the  Accounting  Office
   for activity-related budget and not lump expenses under MOOE account  so
   as both  offices  shall  use  uniform  expense  accounts  for  the  same
   transactions.
